channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu is struggling to prove he’s not an AI clone
item.description: Social media platforms are currently awash with conspiracy theories claiming that Benjamin Netanyahu has been killed or injured and replaced by AI-generated deepfakes. Between clips that supposedly show the Israeli Prime Minister sporting extra fingers and drinking from a bottomless, gravity-defying cup of coffee, only one thing is apparent: reality used to be much easier […]...

item.title: Teens Sue xAI Over Sexualized Images Generated by Grok
item.description: Sexualized images of the girls, created by Grok, were allegedly shared across Discord and Telegram groups.

item.title: We Met Disney’s Most Advanced Robot Yet: Olaf From ‘Frozen’
item.description: The life-size, walking, talking robot will debut in parks later this month, representing huge leaps for Disney.

item.title: Nvidia says China’s BYD and Geely will use its robotaxi platform
item.description: Nvidia added two leading Chinese automakers, BYD and Geely, to its robotaxi program, as the chipmaker seeks to put its stamp on the growing autonomous vehicle market worldwide. At its GTX conference today, Nvidia announced that BYD and Geely, as well as Isuzu and Nissan, would use the chipmaker's Drive Hyperion platform, which combines the […]

item.title: I met Olaf — the Frozen robot who might be the future of Disney Parks
item.description: You know Olaf. Before K-Pop Demon Hunters, before Wicked, it was Disney's Frozen that blasted show tunes like "Let it Go" and "Into the Unknown" into our lives. My little girls loved belting those tunes. So when I met Olaf, the Disney Imagineering robot, I kept thinking: I can't wait for my kids to meet […]

item.title: Memories.ai is building the visual memory layer for wearables and robotics
item.description: Memories.ai is building a large visual memory model that can index and retrieve video-recorded memories for physical AI.

item.title: Nvidia introduces Vera Rubin, a seven-chip AI platform with OpenAI, Anthropic and Meta on board
item.description: Nvidia on Monday took the wraps off Vera Rubin, a sweeping new computing platform built from seven chips now in full production — and backed by an extraordinary lineup of customers that includes Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta and Mistral AI, along with every major cloud provider.The message to the AI industry, and to investors, was unmistakable: Nvidia is not slowing down. The Vera Rubin platform claims up to 10x more inference throughput per watt and one-tenth the cost per token compared with the Blackwell systems that only recently began shipping. CEO Jensen Huang, speaking at the company's annual ...

item.title: Nvidia BlueField-4 STX adds a context memory layer to storage to close the agentic AI throughput gap
item.description: When an AI agent loses context mid-task because traditional storage can't keep pace with inference, it is not a model problem — it is a storage problem. At GTC 2026, Nvidia announced BlueField-4 STX, a modular reference architecture that inserts a dedicated context memory layer between GPUs and traditional storage, claiming 5x the token throughput, 4x the energy efficiency and 2x the data ingestion speed of conventional CPU-based storage.The bottleneck STX targets is key-value cache data. KV cache is the stored record of what a model has already processed — the intermediate calculations an LLM...

item.title: Nvidia's DGX Station is a desktop supercomputer that runs trillion-parameter AI models without the cloud
item.description: Nvidia on Monday unveiled a deskside supercomputer powerful enough to run AI models with up to one trillion parameters — roughly the scale of GPT-4 — without touching the cloud. The machine, called the DGX Station, packs 748 gigabytes of coherent memory and 20 petaflops of compute into a box that sits next to a monitor, and it may be the most significant personal computing product since the original Mac Pro convinced creative professionals to abandon workstations.The announcement, made at the company's annual GTC conference in San Jose, lands at a moment when the AI industry is grappling with ...

item.title: 'Four months' worth of work' — Disney Imagineering built a walking Olaf robot and imagines 'an entire world populated with characters you know and love'
item.description: TechRadar spoke with Kyle Laughlin, SVP of R&D, Technology and Engineering at Walt Disney Imagineering, about how Disney built a walking Olaf robot in just four months — and why it could lead to parks filled with roaming characters.

item.title: DLSS 5 : comment Nvidia exploite le rendu neuronal pour métamorphoser vos jeux PC
item.description: Le DLSS 5 de Nvidia se montre en amont de son lancement officiel en automne prochain. Voici comment fonctionne techniquement cette évolution majeure du DLSS qui promet de changer fondamentalement le rendu de nos jeux sur PC.

item.title: What's New in the AirPods Max 2? Breaking Down Apple's Headphone Update
item.description: Apple's premium over-ears finally have a proper successor, but after five years of waiting, it's hard not to wonder: "Is that it?"

item.title: Sony's enhanced PSSR upscaling arrives on PS5 Pro today
item.description: Sony's upgraded PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR) tech is rolling out as part of the PlayStation 5 Pro system update that's available today. The company had teased last month that this update was in the works. These improvements should be a better reflection of why you might pay a premium price for the more powerful console if you value peak image quality in gaming.For a very surface-level definition, PSSR is Sony's upscaling tech. It uses an AI library for a pixel-by-pixel analysis to display a game with better visuals even while running at a lower resolution. Today's update revamp...

item.title: xAI is being sued by teens who say Grok created CSAM using their photos
item.description: xAI, which is already facing multiple investigations around the world over widespread reports that Grok repeatedly created sexualized images of children, is now facing a class action lawsuit. Three teenagers, who allege that photos of them were used by Grok to generate child exploitation material, have filed a class action lawsuit against xAI in California. According to the lawsuit, one of the teens was alerted last December that someone was sharing AI-generated images and videos of her and other minors "in settings with which she was familiar, but morphed into sexually explicit poses." The im...

item.title: Samsung bets this island startup can tame the grid with software and batteries
item.description: GridBeyond's hardware and software coordinates several gigawatts of supply and demand to help balance the flow of electricity on the grid. The idea has attracted investors like Samsung Ventures.

item.title: What is the release date for Scrubs season 10 episode 5 on Hulu and Disney+?
item.description: Scrubs season 10 reaches its midpoint with this week's episode — here's when and where you can watch it.

item.title: Core Ultra 200S Plus : Intel rafraichit son offre desktop pour les joueurs et les créateurs
item.description: Plutôt que de s'endormir sur ses lauriers, Intel met à jour régulièrement son offre en matière de processeurs pour ordinateurs de bureau. Derniers en date, les Core Ultra 7 270K Plus et Core Ultra 5 250K Plus, deux modèles performants lancés il y a quelques jours à peine. Ils seront disponibles sur le marché d'ici la fin du mois.

item.title: Cette manette pliable Asus pour jouer sur smartphone Android ou Apple ne dépasse pas 80 € pendant les ventes flash d’Amazon
item.description: La manette pour smartphone Asus ROG Tessen Mobile Controller a un petit truc en plus par rapport à ses concurrentes : elle est pliable. Un petit outil très pratique, donc, que l'on trouve à 79,99 euros au lieu de 119,99 euros en ce dernier jour des ventes flash de printemps d'Amazon.

item.title: NVIDIA claims DLSS 5 will deliver 'photoreal' image quality with AI this fall
item.description: Just months after announcing DLSS 4.5 at CES, NVIDIA has unveiled its next major upscaling technology, DLSS 5. The company is doubling-down on AI for this next iteration, claiming DLSS 5 “infuses pixels with photoreal lighting and materials” using a real-time neural rendering model when it arrives this fall. So what does this mean in practice? In an on-stage demo at NVIDIA’s GTC 2026 keynote, CEO Jensen Huang showed off the technology with Resident Evil: Requiem, Hogwarts Legacy and Starfield. DLSS 5 adds a noticeable amount of detail to character’s hair and skin tone, but it also appears it’s...

item.title: This Alien Planet Might Be the Stinkiest Place in the Galaxy
item.description: The molten planet, with an atmosphere rich in sulfur-bearing gases, is unlike anything astronomers have ever smelled.

item.title: Now that's personal AI — Claude can now turn your handwriting into a personal font, giving you extra ways to express yourself
item.description: Experiments with Claude show scanned handwriting can become a font file, though detection errors and messy writing still cause problems.

item.title: Apple acquires video editing software company MotionVFX
item.description: The move could help Apple better compete with Adobe Premiere Pro and the Adobe Creative Cloud suite.

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI faces child porn lawsuit from minors Grok allegedly undressed
item.description: The three plaintiffs are seeking to represent anyone who had real images of them as a minor altered into sexual content by Grok.

item.title: 'This is no normal mini PC': Minisforum MS-S1 Max with AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 CPU, 128GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2TB SSD is the best mini workstation for AI we’ve ever tested - and it's now on sale at Newegg
item.description: Minisforum’s MS-S1 Max is powered by the AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor for AI workloads and demanding professional tasks.

item.title: Animated ‘Firefly’ Already Exists, and It’s Called ‘Outlaw Star’
item.description: The sky's already been taken from you to much better effect in Sunrise's 1998 sci-fi anime.

item.title: z.ai debuts faster, cheaper GLM-5 Turbo model for agents and 'claws' — but it's not open-source
item.description: Chinese AI startup Z.ai, known for its powerful, open source GLM family of large language models (LLMs), has introduced GLM-5-Turbo, a new, proprietary variant of its open source GLM-5 model aimed at agent-driven workflows, with the company positioning it as a faster model tuned for OpenClaw-style tasks such as tool use, long-chain execution and persistent automation. It's available now through Z.ai's application programming interface (API) on third-party provider OpenRouter with roughly a 202.8K-token context window, 131.1K max output, and listed pricing of $0.96 per million input tokens and ...

item.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 uses generative AI to boost photorealism in video games, with ambitions beyond gaming
item.description: Nvidia’s new DLSS 5 uses generative AI and structured graphics data to make video games more realistic. CEO Jensen Huang says the approach could eventually spread to other industries.

item.title: Pourquoi Asus quitte définitivement le marché du smartphone en 2026 ?
item.description: Asus met fin à sa gamme de smartphones, aussi bien les Zenfone que les ROG Phone. Une annonce faite par son président Jonney Shih, marquant un tournant pour une marque pourtant réputée pour son audace. Nous revenons, en vidéo, sur ce que cette disparition raconte du marché actuel du smartphone.

item.title: This Core Ultra 7 laptop deal for professionals with a 2K OLED 120Hz display is £427 off — Acer Swift Go 16 is just £722 with a code
item.description: Acer’s Swift Go pairs an Intel Core Ultra 7 255H processor with 16GB RAM and a 1TB SSD for demanding work, fast multitasking, and AI-powered applications.

item.title: Polymarket Bettors Allegedly Send Israeli Reporter Death Threats Demanding Changes to Article
item.description: That's not how being right works, guys.

item.title: Save Almost 20 Percent On Our Favorite Portable Bluetooth Speaker
item.description: The Tribit Stormbox Mini+ is as affordable as it is compact.

item.title: Energy hungry Big Tech turn to carbon credits to fuel data centers while remaining committed to net-zero emissions
item.description: AI is likely to blame for rising Big Tech emissions, and companies are mostly offsetting those with carbon credits.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6, aspirateur robot DJI Romo P et méga promos Xiaomi : les 3 bons plans tech immanquables du lundi 16 mars 2026
item.description: Les Ventes Flash de Printemps touchent à leur fin, mais les réductions impressionnantes continuent ce lundi 16 mars 2026.

item.title: Judge rules that Krafton must rehire fired Subnautica director
item.description: A judge has ruled that publisher Krafton must reinstate Ted Gill as CEO of Unknown Worlds Entertainment, according to a report by Bloomberg. The company fired Gill and two other co-founders last year as part of a shakeup involving the long-anticipated sequel Subnautica 2. The Delaware judge said Krafton had violated the terms of its contract with Unknown Worlds when it fired the executives. "To remedy these breaches, Gill is reinstated as CEO of Unknown Worlds with full operational authority over the studio," wrote judge Lori W. Will. A Krafton spokesperson said in a statement that "we respect...

item.title: FCC Chairman Threatens to Revoke TV Licenses Over Iran Coverage
item.description: Here's the latest in the FCC's quest to keep American media on a tight leash.

item.title: Fini les problèmes visuels sur la PS5 Pro ? Sony livre une importante mise à jour
item.description: Après des débuts compliqués, le PSSR de la PS5 Pro tente de se refaire une santé. Sony a dévoilé une nouvelle version de sa technologie d’upscaling, peaufinée avec AMD, et promet enfin une image plus stable et plus précise.
